behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names all file paths (.claude/skills/changeset/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/pull-request/SKILL.md, justfile, .claude/skills/testing-codegen/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/biome-developer/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/README.md), required frontmatter fields (name, description, compatibility), required section headings (Purpose, Changeset Format, Writing Guidelines, AI Assistance Disclosure, Choose the Target Branch, PR Title, Pre-PR Checklist), the recipe name 'new-changeset-empty', the command

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes WHAT to create (new skill files, new justfile recipe, modifications to existing files) and the structural requirements, but does not give exact file contents, line numbers to change, or verbatim text to insert. A developer must still reason about the actual content and implementation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for structural properties (frontmatter fields, section headings via regex patterns, presence of keywords like 'patch'/'minor'/'major', recipe name and body keywords) rather than exact text. Alternative implementations with different wording, ordering, or additional content would pass as long as they meet the structural requirements.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All tests read files and check text content via regex/string matching (e.g., checking frontmatter YAML parsing, searching for heading patterns, checking keyword presence). No test actually executes the justfile recipe, imports code, or runs any behavioral subprocess. Tests like test_justfile_new_changeset_empty_recipe only check that 'pnpm', 'changeset', and '--empty' appear in the recipe body text, not that the recipe actually runs.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests require creating new files (changeset/SKILL.md, pull-request/SKILL.md) with specific frontmatter and sections, adding a new justfile recipe with specific content, and modifying existing files. A stub/empty implementation would not pass since tests check for file existence, valid YAML frontmatter with specific field values, required section headings, and specific keywords in content.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction specifies 6 main tasks and the tests cover all of them: new changeset skill (frontmatter, sections, writing guidelines, change types, just command reference), new pull-request skill (frontmatter, required sections), Code Comments in biome-developer (DO/DON'T, WRONG/CORRECT examples), new-changeset-empty recipe in justfile, testing-codegen cleanup (no changeset mention, reference to changeset skill), and README updates (new entries in table and directory tree).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are clearly separated into pass-to-pass and fail-to-pass groups with comments, each has a descriptive name (e.g., test_changeset_skill_has_writing_guidelines, test_pull_request_skill_valid_frontmatter) and docstrings explaining what they verify.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task produces structured Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. The instruction documents the exact schema: required frontmatter fields (name, description, compatibility), required section headings, and content requirements for each file.

no_typos.reason: File paths, commands, variable names, and section headings in the instruction are consistent with what the tests check. No typos detected in filenames (.claude/skills/changeset/SKILL.md, justfile, etc.), commands (pnpm changeset --empty, just new-changeset-empty), or field names.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies what files to create/modify and what sections they should contain, which is appropriate for a documentation/configuration task. It doesn't reveal implementation-specific details like exact line numbers or verbatim content to paste.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold patch makes localized changes: adding a recipe to justfile via sed insertion, creating new skill files, and modifying specific sections in existing files. Changes don't contradict the test expectations or other task files.
